# **Organic Chemistry**

## **Carbon Foundations and Bonding**

* Carbon is the L0 central element of organic chemistry due to its L1 ability to form four stable covalent bonds | Atomic Theory | 0.98  
* Catenation is the L0 ability of carbon atoms to link together into long L1 chains or rings | Chemical Bonding | 0.97  
* Hybridization (![][image1]) is the L1 model explaining the L0 geometric arrangement of carbon bonds | Quantum Chemistry | 0.96  
* Functional Groups are specific L0 clusters of atoms that determine the L1 chemical properties of a molecule | Classification | 0.97  
* Isomerism occurs when molecules have the same L1 molecular formula but different L0 structural arrangements | Molecular Logic | 0.95

## **Reaction Mechanisms**

* Nucleophiles are L0 electron-rich species that donate electron pairs to form L1 bonds | Reaction Theory | 0.96  
* Electrophiles are L0 electron-deficient species that accept electron pairs to form L1 bonds | Reaction Theory | 0.96  
* Resonance is the L1 model describing delocalized electrons in molecules, increasing L1 stability | Molecular Physics | 0.95  
* Steric Hindrance is the L0 physical congestion that prevents L1 chemical reactions from occurring | Reaction Kinetics | 0.94  
* Electronegativity differences drive L1 bond polarity, a dependency for all L0 reaction predictions | General Chemistry | 0.97

## **Organic Meta-Context**

* Chiral molecules are L0 non-superimposable mirror images, an L1 geometric property crucial for L0 pharmacology | Pharmacology | 0.96  
* Polymerization is the L1 process of linking L0 monomers into L1 macromolecules, shared with Biochemistry | Cross-Domain | 0.97  
* Aromaticity (Hückel's Rule) provides an L1 stability bonus to L0 cyclic, planar molecules | Advanced Theory | 0.94  
* Metabolic pathways in Biochemistry are complex sequences of L0 organic reactions regulated by L1 enzymes | Biochemistry | 0.95
